prevalence.value: Low in general population; concentrated in historically exposed worker cohorts
prevalence.population: Adults with past occupational asbestos exposure
prevalence.source: Occupational lung disease epidemiology syntheses; WHO asbestos-related disease context
prevalence.trend: slowly decreasing in new cases; persistent burden due to long latency

incidence.value: Low; long latency (10-40+ years) after exposure

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; high in affected cohorts with progressive fibrosis
burden.mortalityRate: Moderate in severe cases; also elevated mesothelioma/lung cancer risk (related exposure)
burden.source: WHO/IHME occupational risk and chronic respiratory disease context

vulnerablePopulations: communities near asbestos use/processing sites (lower risk)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: History of asbestos exposure plus interstitial fibrosis pattern on imaging (HRCT), restrictive spirometry, and exclusion of other ILDs; assess pleural plaques

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Limited (irreversible fibrosis); management focuses on symptom control and prevention of progression/complications

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Pulmonology evaluation with HRCT and spirometry; occupational history often under-documented
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Smoking cessation, vaccinations, pulmonary rehabilitation, oxygen when needed, treat comorbid COPD; surveillance for malignancy as clinically indicated
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Under-recognition, access to HRCT and occupational medicine services, legacy exposures with long latency
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Clear prevention pathway via exposure control; supportive care available in pulmonology services
